title: Shoals Technologies Group Q2 2022 Earnings Call Summary
text: Shoals Technologies delivered strong results in Q2 2022, with revenue reaching $73.5 million, representing 23% year-over-year growth. This marked the sixth consecutive quarter of record revenue and gross profit. Gross profit increased 9% to $28.6 million with gross margin of 38.9% (compared to 43.8% prior year). Adjusted EBITDA was $19.8 million (vs. $20.6 million prior year). Backlog and awarded orders reached $327.2 million, up 63% year-over-year. System Solutions (BLA) generated 77% of revenue, growing 11% year-over-year. The company converted four additional EPCs to their System Solutions platform in Q2, with 29 total new BLA customer adds. Components revenue surged 97% year-over-year, driven by battery storage shipments and new customer onboarding in solar products. The company introduced several new products gaining traction: Wire management products (backlog increased 3x since year-start), Energy storage solutions, and EV charging infrastructure for commercial fleets and school buses. Management noted that customer demand is exceeding expectations in the EV business segment. The company reaffirmed 2022 guidance: Revenue $300-325 million (41-52% growth), Adjusted EBITDA $77-86 million, Capital expenditures $7-8 million. The company announced Dominic Bardos as incoming CFO starting Q4 2022, bringing over 30 years of finance experience from automotive and industrial sectors.
